title: Flea Treatment in New Brunswick, NJ | $350-$650 | Best Pest Control NYC
metaDescription: Professional flea treatment services in New Brunswick, NJ starting at $350. Fast, effective flea extermination for homes and businesses. Same-day service available.
intro: Dealing with a flea infestation in New Brunswick, NJ? Best Pest Control NYC offers comprehensive flea treatment services designed to eliminate fleas at every life stage. Our experienced technicians serve residential and commercial properties throughout New Brunswick, from historic neighborhoods near Rutgers University to modern apartments along the Raritan River.
whyUs: We combine advanced treatment methods with local expertise to deliver effective flea control throughout New Brunswick. Our licensed technicians understand the unique pest challenges in Central Jersey and provide same-day service with guaranteed results at competitive rates of $350-$650.
localContext: New Brunswick's mix of university housing, historic Victorian homes, and waterfront developments creates diverse environments where fleas can thrive, especially in pet-friendly buildings and multi-unit residences. The city's humid climate and dense population near Albany Street and George Street corridors make professional flea treatment essential for maintaining pest-free living and working spaces.

faqs.question: How much does flea treatment cost in New Brunswick, NJ?
faqs.answer: Flea treatment in New Brunswick typically costs between $350 and $650, depending on the size of your property, severity of the infestation, and number of rooms requiring treatment. We provide free inspections and upfront pricing before beginning any work.

faqs.question: How long does flea treatment take to work in New Brunswick homes?
faqs.answer: Most flea treatments show significant results within 24-48 hours, with complete elimination typically achieved within 2-3 weeks as the treatment targets adult fleas, larvae, and eggs through their entire life cycle. We recommend follow-up treatments to ensure all stages are eliminated.

faqs.question: Are flea treatments safe for pets and children in New Brunswick?
faqs.answer: Yes, we use EPA-approved products that are safe for pets and children when applied correctly. We recommend keeping pets and children away from treated areas for 2-4 hours after application until surfaces are dry, then they can safely return to treated spaces.

faqs.question: Do you service Rutgers University area apartments for flea treatment?
faqs.answer: Absolutely! We regularly service apartments, dormitories, and rental properties throughout the Rutgers campus area and all New Brunswick neighborhoods. We work with landlords, property managers, and individual tenants to provide discreet, effective flea control services.
